summary: Disambiguate how to bring external/other data into Fabric: mirroring (continuous replication) vs shortcut (in-place reference, no copy) vs copy-job (one-time move) vs dataflow (low-code transform). They look similar but have very different semantics.

meanings.context: Continuous replication of an external DB
meanings.artifact: Mirrored Database / Catalog (Snowflake, Databricks UC, Cosmos, SQL)
meanings.description: Continuously replicates a source into OneLake Delta; read-only in Fabric, queried via the SQL analytics endpoint. Use when you want a live, queryable copy.
Points at external storage (ADLS, S3, Dataverse, OneLake) without copying. Use when you want to query in place and avoid duplication.
example_commands: fabio shortcut create --workspace $WS --id $LH --path Files/ext --name data --target adlsgen2 --location ... --key ...

meanings.description: Moves data once (or on a schedule) between Fabric items. Use for a bulk move, not continuous sync.
Power Query ETL that transforms as it loads. Use when the data needs shaping, not just replication.

default_action: Live queryable copy of an external DB -> mirroring. Reference in place -> shortcut. One-time move -> copy-job. Transform while ingesting -> dataflow.
